items.title: PM sends aid to 10 Sabah households hit by fire
items.summary: The assistance was presented by Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Sabah and Sarawak Affairs) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud. "Today, I, along with officers from the Prime Minister's Department's Implementation Coordination Unit, represented the prime minister to present contributions to the victims of the fire incident. "The prime minister also conveyed his condolences to all the victims involved," he said.

items.title: Cost of diesel subsidies have tripled to RM2.2b from RM700m, says domestic trade minister
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 5 — The monthly allocation for the Subsidised Diesel Control Scheme (SKDS) is estimated to have risen to RM2.2 billion in March, nearly three times higher than about RM700 million previously, following a sharp increase in global diesel prices. Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the surge in expenditure was driven by escalating global diesel prices. "Under the SKDS, a total of 33 types of vehicles are eligible for subsidised diesel, comprising 10 categories of public land transport vehicles and 23 categories of goods transport vehicles.

items.title: Family buries Mexican who died in US migrant detention
items.summary: SAN JUAN CHAMULA: Hundreds of people gathered Saturday for the burial of a young Mexican man who died in US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) custody, with family members denouncing the "fabricated" charges that led to his detention and death. Royer Perez Jimenez, a 19-year-old from the municipality of San Juan Chamula in the southern Mexican state of Chiapas, died on March 16 at a detention centre in Florida. ICE officials said Royer died of an apparent suicide but the official cause of death remained under investigation.

items.title: 2 hikers rescued after being stranded at Lambir Hills in Sibu
items.summary: MIRI: The Fire and Rescue Department rescued two hikers stranded at Lambir Hills National Park early this morning. Lambir Hills National Park, a short drive from Miri, is a biodiverse rainforest with hiking trails, waterfalls, and scenic nature spots. The operations centre received an emergency call at 6.30pm yesterday reporting three hikers stranded in the park.

items.title: Chan says vegetable price slumps are 'normal'
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Vegetable price fluctuations are largely driven by supply and demand dynamics, Deputy Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Chan Foong Hin said, noting that prices often ease after festive periods such as Hari Raya Aidilfitri. On concerns over fertiliser export restrictions by other countries, Chan said domestic supply remains stable despite global uncertainties. Chan said the Agro Madani initiative continues to benefit consumers by offering produce at lower prices.

items.title: Thunderstorms and heavy rain to hit five states until 4pm today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds affecting five states until 4pm today. MetMalaysia, in a statement, said the affected areas in Pahang are Bentong and Rompin, while in Negri Sembilan, they include Jelebu and Kuala Pilah. A similar weather warning has also been issued for Sarawak, involving Kuching (Bau and Kuching), Serian (Serian), Samarahan (Samarahan and Simunjan), Sri Aman (Sri Aman), Sarikei (Julau and Meradong), Sibu, Mukah, Kapit (Song, Kapit and Bukit Mabong) and Bintulu (Tatau).

items.title: 'We're barely surviving': Lojing farmers face collapse as prices crash
items.summary: GUA MUSANG: Farmers in the Lojing Highlands say they are being pushed to the brink of halting operations as falling prices, weak demand and rising costs squeeze their livelihoods. Other produce has also seen sharp declines, including tomatoes (RM3 to 60 sen), cabbage (RM3 to 60 sen), Chinese celery leaves (RM15 to 80 sen), mustard greens (RM3 to 80 sen) and kailan (RM3 to 80 sen). As a result, locally grown produce is sold at extremely low prices because imports are not regulated," he said.

items.title: Fishermen say 50 litre fuel subsidy falls short of daily needs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The 50 litre daily fuel subsidy for Zone A fishermen is no longer sufficient for current operations, especially for those using seine and longline trawl methods nationwide. Malaysian Coastal Fishermen's Network Education and Welfare Association deputy chairman Mohd Faizal Mohd Zabri said most fishermen now operate boats with engines up to 200 horsepower (hp), compared with around 85hp previously. He added that larger engines have increased fuel consumption per trip, particularly for longer coastal journeys.

items.title: Man arrested after security guard beaten to death in Klang housing estate, car lock seized
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — A security guard has died after being assaulted at a residential estate in Bandar Bukit Raja, Klang, with police arresting a man believed to be responsible hours later. North Klang district police chief Assistant Commissioner S. Vijaya Rao said the attack was reported at about 1am at a guardhouse in the housing area today. Police later tracked down the suspect and seized a car lock believed to have been used in the assault.

items.title: Diesel pickup owners hold back as resale prices plunge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pickup truck owners are reluctant to sell as diesel resale prices fall, with dealers offering valuations many consider unreasonably low, deterring sales in the used market. The situation comes as diesel prices reach RM6.02 per litre, prompting many owners to delay selling their vehicles to avoid heavy losses, Kosmo! Transporter service operator Nik Muhammad Koidin Nik Mustafa, 42, said he has decided not to sell his vehicles, citing weak market prices.

items.title: Dead on paper, alive in KL: Man’s 17-year fight to prove he’s no ghost in the system
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — For nearly two decades, Mohd Rosli Idris has lived a life few can imagine — alive in person, but officially dead on paper. Rosli, who was then working as a second-hand clothing seller around Chow Kit, said the mix-up began when his former employer encountered a gravely ill man who closely resembled him. With no identification on the man, the employer contacted Rosli’s cousin.

items.title: Rescue ends as second teen's body recovered from Sungai Pahang
items.summary: JERANTUT: A 14-year-old teenager, who was among the two victims reported missing while bathing in Sungai Pahang at Kampung Durian Hijau here on April 3, was found drowned today. Muhammad Amirul Ashraf Abdullah's remains were found at 9.50am on the third day of the search and rescue (SAR) operation. Pahang Fire and Rescue Department assistant director (Operations) Mohd Salahuddin Isa said Amirul Ashraf's body was spotted by members of the public about three kilometres from the location where he was reported missing on April 3.

items.title: Johor allocates RM1.59mil to replace roofs at storm-hit Kulai flats
items.summary: KULAI: The Johor government has approved RM1.59 million to replace roofs at three residential blocks in Flat Taman Puteri following a severe storm yesterday. State Housing and Local Government Committee chairman Datuk Mohd Jafni Md Shukor said works will begin on April 10 and involve replacing ageing asbestos roofs with more durable metal deck structures. It was reported that eight units at Flat Taman Puteri were affected, including six that sustained severe roof damage.

items.title: 5E Resources poised to ride ESG, manufacturing growth wave
items.summary: Affin Hwang Investment Bank Bhd said these factors are expected to drive long-term demand for scheduled waste management services. The Johor-based integrated scheduled waste management service provider is licenced to handle 34 scheduled waste categories and is expanding its capacity by 80 per cent in 2026 to serve a diverse customer base. "The scheduled waste management industry in Malaysia is regulation-driven and highly compliance-intensive, with demand supported by industrial activity, rising ESG adoption and circular economy initiatives," the firm said.

items.title: Mount Kinabalu’s Unesco World Heritage status not up for trade in cable car debate, says Sabah DCM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — There can be no compromise on the conditions underpinning Mount Kinabalu’s Unesco World Heritage status, Sabah’s deputy chief minister has said, as a proposed cable car project remains under study. Datuk Seri Masidi Manjun said any development around the mountain must prioritise environmental preservation and its heritage value, warning against viewing the proposal purely through a commercial lens. However, we must not forget that Mount Kinabalu is a World Heritage Site.

items.title: Suspect in MRT Kajang harassment case arrested
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have arrested a 24-year-old man in connection with an alleged sexual harassment incident at the MRT Kajang station that went viral on social media. Kajang police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said the arrest was made on April 4 at the suspect's residence in Taman West Country, Bandar Baru Bangi. He said the case surfaced following the circulation of a 23-second video on the Threads application on April 2 at 6.15pm, which described an incident at Gate A of the MRT Kajang station in Selangor.

items.title: 'I'm still alive!': Man spends 17 years proving he's not a ghost
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 54-year-old man has been struggling to lead a normal life and find work after he was mistakenly declared dead 17 years ago. Still alive, 54-year-old Mohd Rosli Idris has been trapped in a bureaucratic error that erased his identity from official records, Kosmo! Rosli has lived without valid identification, denying him the right to work, access aid and live a normal life like other Malaysians.

items.title: Injured man among two stranded hikers rescued after overnight operation in Sarawak national park
items.summary: SIBU, April 5 — The Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) search and rescue team rescued two hikers who were stranded at Lambir Hills National Park early this morning. “The two hikers rescued early this morning were identified as Gordon Brian, 32, who sustained a leg injury, and Wan Mohamad Hidayat, 42, who was unhurt,” the statement read. The operations centre had earlier received an emergency call at 6.30pm yesterday reporting that three hikers were stranded in the national park.

items.title: Johor rejects '40,000 SMEs at risk' claim as political rhetoric
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Johor government has dismissed claims that 40,000 small and medium enterprises (SMEs) in the state are at risk of closure due to the Middle East crisis, describing the assertion as politically driven and unsupported by data. State Youth, Sports, Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Committee chairman Mohd Hairi Mad Shah said the narrative, recently raised by Johor Bersatu and the Johor Bumiputera Entrepreneur Council (Teraju), lacked credible evidence. He warned that such claims could mislead both the public and the business community, particularly given Johor's distinct economic position bordering Singapore.

items.title: Police arrest suspect in Kajang MRT station sexual harassment case after viral clip
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — A man suspected of committing an act of sexual harassment at Kajang MRT station, in an incident that went viral on the Threads app, has been arrested, police said yesterday. Kajang district police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said the 24-year-old man was detained at his home in Bandar Baru Bangi, Kajang. Earlier, media reports said police were tracking down a man believed to have committed an act of sexual harassment at the Kajang MRT station exit, following the circulation of the video on Threads.

items.title: Gold futures likely to see cautious trading next week
items.summary: On a week-on-week basis, the spot-month April 2026 contract rose to US$4,670.00 per troy ounce from US$4,459.80 per troy ounce recorded in the previous week. The May 2026 contract improved to US$4,689.30 per troy ounce from US$4,479.80 per troy ounce. The June, July, and August 2026 contracts also finished firmer at US$4,708.60 per troy ounce from US$4,513.70 per troy ounce in the preceding week.

items.title: South Korea asks Gulf nations for steady energy supply, safety of Korean vessels
items.summary: SEOUL: South Korean Finance Minister Koo Yun-cheol met with envoys from Gulf countries to shore up energy security and the safety of Korean vessels near the Strait of Hormuz, the ministry said on Sunday, as the escalating Iran war disrupts shipping. In the meeting on Friday, Koo asked the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) ambassadors to ensure a steady supply of oil, liquefied natural gas, naphtha, urea and other critical resources, and to ensure the safety of Korean vessels and crew near the vital strait, the ministry said in a statement. The envoys said South Korea is a "top priority" nation and pledged to communicate closely with Seoul to ensure stable supply, the statement said.

items.title: Don’t cut frontline fuel: Ex-minister moots 300-litre subsidy carve-out for healthcare workers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — A targeted petrol subsidy of up to 300 litres for healthcare workers should be considered, particularly those carrying out regular field duties, Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa has said. The Backbenchers Club chairman said some public healthcare staff rely on their own vehicles to visit chronic patients, monitor infectious diseases and carry out community health interventions. However, she said a targeted approach could ensure healthcare workers with high mobility needs continue to receive support without undermining subsidy rationalisation.

items.title: Terrifying storm tears roofs off homes, displaces 6 families in Johor
items.summary: KULAI: A sudden 15-minute storm tore the roofs off six homes at Blok 4, Flat Temenggong here yesterday, leaving six families displaced. Wong, who met the 18 victims with Kulai member of parliament Teo Nie Ching, said emergency aid had been distributed. Falling debris also damaged 10 cars.

items.title: From Sabah to Sarawak: St John Ambulans Malaysia pulls off complex 600km emergency transfer
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 5 — St John Ambulans Malaysia (SJAM) Sabah completed a medical transfer covering more than 600km from here to Bintulu, Sarawak, on April 1. State Superintendent Jansen Sai told Daily Express that the operation underscored the organisation’s capability in executing long-distance emergency medical services. He said the mission was led by State Commander Ng Ming Syoung, with support from First Aider Joe and State Staff Nursing Officer Janet.

items.title: Former IAEA head labels Trump a 'madman' for launching bombing campaign
items.summary: CAIRO: A former director of the United Nations nuclear watchdog urged Gulf nations on Saturday to prevent Donald Trump from turning the region into "a ball of fire" after the US president's latest threat to Teheran. In a separate post also referencing Trump's ultimatum to Iran that it will face "all hell" unless it strikes a deal, El-Baradei tagged UN and European Union accounts, as well as French President Emmanuel Macron. Trump said Saturday that Iran must make a deal or reopen the vital Strait of Hormuz, which Iran has effectively blocked since US-Israeli strikes on Teheran on Feb 28 sparked a larger regional war.

items.title: Hamzah teases shift without moving: Opposition Leader hints at PAS-linked ‘new home’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — Opposition Leader Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin has deepened the intrigue over his political future, suggesting his much-talked-about “new home” may not be new at all, but an existing party already within the Perikatan Nasional (PN) coalition, according to Sinar Harian . “When we say a new home, does it mean a new party or an existing party? “Yes… it could be an existing party.

items.title: Indonesia receives bodies of peacekeepers killed in Lebanon [WATCH]
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia received the bodies of three peacekeepers on Saturday who were killed while on deployment in Lebanon and called for security guarantees for blue helmets a day after another three of its soldiers were injured. Another three Indonesian peacekeepers were injured in Lebanon Friday, two seriously, officials said. Foreign Minister Sugiono told reporters after Saturday's ceremony that Indonesia wanted a thorough UN investigation.

items.title: 'Don't bomb': Israelis call for ending Iran war
items.summary: TEL AVIV: Carrying anti-war banners and chanting slogans against Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, hundreds of Israelis rallied in Tel Aviv on Saturday to protest the war with Iran. End the endless war!" "In Israel, there's always a war.

items.title: 'No two camps in PKR,' says Ramanan amid leadership differences
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: PKR vice-president Datuk Seri R Ramanan has dismissed claims of internal factionalism, insisting the party remains united under a single leadership despite recent public disagreements among its leaders. There is only one team under PKR president Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim," he said. Ramanan said the party's primary focus remains on maintaining the stability of the unity government and safeguarding public welfare under Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's leadership.

items.title: PNB kicks off Jelajah Warisan KL at Stadium Merdeka
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Permodalan Nasional Bhd's subsidiary PNB Merdeka Ventures Sdn Bhd has launched the first Jelajah Warisan KL at Stadium Merdeka. The initiative, part of the broader Warisan Kuala Lumpur agenda, involves government agencies, government-linked companies (GLCs), government-linked investment companies (GLICs), heritage partners and local communities. PNB Merdeka chief executive officer Datuk Izwan Ibrahim said Jelajah Warisan Kuala Lumpur is more than a guided walk.

items.title: Motorcyclist killed in six-vehicle crash in Kota Baru
items.summary: KOTA BARU: A motorcyclist was killed in an accident involving six vehicles in front of Restoran Pok Dik, along Jalan Sultan Yahya Petra, just after midnight yesterday. He said that before the incident, a car was travelling from Wakaf Siku towards Kubang Kerian. "The car is believed to have been speeding and lost control before skidding onto the left shoulder of the road towards Kubang Kerian, where it struck a motorcyclist who was stationary on the left shoulder.

items.title: Search teams recover second boy’s body after Jerantut river tragedy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — The body of 14-year-old Muhammad Amirul Ashraf Abdullah, a student of Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Jengka 24, was found by search and rescue (SAR) teams in Jerantut at about 9.50am today. According to Berita Harian , Jerantut fire station chief Raja Arif Raja Shamsudin said the body was recovered and brought ashore by rescuers. The body of a second victim, Muhammad Danial Haikal Shamsudin, had been found earlier at about 4.20pm yesterday, roughly 2km from the same location.

items.title: Israeli forces destroy 17 UN peacekeeper cameras in Lebanon: UN
items.summary: BEIRUT: Israeli forces destroyed 17 surveillance cameras linked to the United Nations peacekeepers' main headquarters in southern Lebanon in 24 hours, a UN security official told AFP on Saturday. Since the start of the Israel-Hizbollah war on March 2, the UN Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) has been caught in the crossfire in the country's south, with Hizbollah launching attacks on Israel and its troops, and Israeli forces pushing into border towns. UNIFIL spokeswoman Kandice Ardiel told AFP on Saturday that "the cameras appear to have been destroyed by some kind of laser."

items.title: Kedah’s Kota Setar swelters under Level 2 heatwave as temperatures hit 40°C
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — Parts of northern Malaysia are baking under escalating heat conditions, with Kota Setar in Kedah continuing to record Level 2 heatwave status as temperatures climb as high as 40°C. These include Perlis and several districts across Kedah — Kuala Muda, Kubang Pasu, Pokok Sena, Pendang, Padang Terap, Sik and Baling — signalling widespread heat stress across the northern peninsula. Similar alert-level conditions have also been recorded in parts of Perak, Kelantan, Pahang and Johor, including Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kuala Krai, Pasir Mas, Tanah Merah, Machang, Maran, Jerantut and Segamat.

items.title: Motorcyclist dies in Kota Bharu pile-up after speeding car veers off road, hits four parked vehicles
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — A 41-year-old motorcyclist was killed after a car lost control and ploughed into him before crashing into four parked vehicles in a six-vehicle pile-up in Kota Bharu early today. Kota Bharu district police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Azmir Damiri said officers received a report at 1.10am. “Upon reaching the scene, the car veered onto the left shoulder towards Kubang Kerian and struck a motorcyclist who was stopped by the roadside.

items.title: 3 lorries, pickup collide on DUKE highway, killing 2
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Two men were killed in an accident involving three lorries and a pickup truck at Km10.4 of the Duta-Ulu Kelang Expressway (DUKE), from the Ayer Panas toll heading towards Ampang, yesterday. City Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the incident occurred at 3.43pm. "Following that, another lorry stopped to provide assistance.

items.title: Bomba expands grassroots fire response with 500 volunteer brigade target by 2030
items.summary: BATU KAWAN, April 5 — The Malaysian Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) is optimistic about achieving its target of establishing 500 Volunteer Fire Brigade (PBS) teams nationwide by 2030, in line with its fire services policy and strategic plan. Director-general Datuk Nor Hisham Mohammad said the target is considered realistic based on current momentum, as well as continued commitment from various parties, including state governments and local communities. He was met after the closing ceremony of the PBS Convention and the Penang JBPM Penang Public Safety and Awareness Campaign, officiated by Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow, at the Batu Kawan Stadium last night.

items.title: IMF urges BOJ to keep raising rates even as Iran war poses new risks
items.summary: TOKYO: The International Monetary Fund urged the Bank of Japan to continue raising interest rates, even as the Middle East war posed "significant new risks" to the country's economic outlook. "Risks to the outlook and inflation are broadly balanced," with inflation expected to converge to the BOJ's two per cent target in 2027, the IMF said. Japan's fiscal year starts in April.

items.title: Aaron urges Malaysians to uphold unity and compassion in Easter message
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — National Unity Minister Datuk Aaron Ago Dagang has called on Malaysians to continue fostering interfaith harmony with mutual respect and compassion to ensure the nation’s unity remains intact. In a Facebook post in conjunction with Easter, he said Malaysia is built on diversity and that peace and unity must always remain central among the people. “Happy Easter!

items.title: MAG's hedging strategy pays off amid fuel volatility, eyes China for growth
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG) is strengthening its resilience against fuel price volatility through a structured hedging strategy to counter rising costs, while maintaining a steady expansion trajectory with particular focus on lucrative China and other key Asian markets. Its newly appointed president and group chief executive officer Captain Nasaruddin A Bakar said the airline group is navigating a challenging operating environment marked by rising fuel prices and geopolitical uncertainties, which have significantly increased cost pressures across the aviation industry. Fuel Costs Surge Amid Geopolitical Tensions He said fuel costs, which typically account for about 40 per cent of MAG's total operating expenses, have risen sharply amid the ongoing crisis, now contributing approximately 50 per cent of overall costs.

items.title: Perhilitan’s biggest wildlife bust: Hundreds of bear and python parts seized in RM36.8m Johor raid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — Malaysian authorities have seized RM36.8 million worth of protected wildlife parts — including hundreds of python and bear gall bladders — in what they say is the largest haul of its kind. Perhilitan director-general Datuk Abdul Kadir Abu Hashim said officers moved in at about 9.50pm and discovered a stockpile of wildlife products believed to be destined for the black market. Among the items seized were 602 python gall bladders, 420 bear gall bladders, and dozens of compressed pieces suspected to be parts of tigers and primates.

items.title: Artemis astronauts preparing for historic lunar flyby
items.summary: HOUSTON: The Artemis astronauts were gearing up Saturday for their long-anticipated lunar flyby, including reviewing the surface features they must analyze and photograph during their time circling the Moon. Upon waking around 1635 GMT on Saturday, the astronauts were approximately 169,000 miles (271,979 kilometres) from Earth, and approaching the Moon at 110,700 miles (178,154 kilometres), according to NASA. If all proceeds smoothly, as Orion whips around the Moon the astronauts could set a record by venturing farther from Earth than any human before.

items.title: Listing-bound Golden Destinations set for steady earnings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tourism player Golden Destinations Group Bhd (GD Group), which is heading for an ACE Market listing, is expected to deliver steady earnings growth over the next three years. Malacca Securities Sdn Bhd said this is supported by rising demand for Asia-bound travel, expansion into Singapore and pent-up demand for international travel following disruptions linked to the Middle East conflict. Malacca Securities assigned a fair value of 51 sen per share for Golden Destinations, implying a 13.3 per cent upside from its initial public offering.

items.title: Two killed, three injured in DUKE chain crash after broken-down lorry triggers pile-up
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — Two men were killed in a chain-reaction crash involving three lorries and a pickup truck on the Duta–Ulu Kelang Expressway (DUKE) yesterday afternoon. Kuala Lumpur traffic investigations chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said a pickup travelling behind failed to control its vehicle and slammed into the stationary lorry. “They were a local lorry driver and a Bangladeshi pickup driver.

items.title: Kota Setar in Kedah hit by Level 2 heatwave
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kota Setar in Kedah continues to experience Level 2 heatwave conditions, with daily maximum temperatures ranging from 37 degrees Celsius to 40 degrees Celsius. The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) said early morning readings at 6am showed eight areas at Level One (Alert), with daily highs of 35 degrees Celsius to 37 degrees Celsius. Affected areas include Perlis, Kuala Muda, Kubang Pasu, Pokok Sena, Pendang, Padang Terap, Sik and Baling in Kedah.

items.title: Abdul Rahman Dahlan steps down in Tuaran Umno chief, says time to pass the baton
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — Former Kota Belud MP Datuk Seri Abdul Rahman Dahlan has stepped down as Tuaran Umno division chief, saying the party needs younger leaders to take charge, while retaining his role on Umno’s supreme council. The former federal minister said his resignation, effective April 1, was not driven by internal issues in Sabah Umno but by a need to “give way to new leaders” amid shifting political dynamics. Abdul Rahman said he had informed Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and secretary-general Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki of his decision.

items.title: PAS denies backing Hamzah as Opposition Leader, says successor to be named soon
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — PAS has rejected claims it wants Larut MP Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin to remain Opposition Leader, insisting instead that it will take over the post and name a replacement soon despite a delay in announcing its candidate. The party said it remained firm on assuming the role, with an announcement expected before the next Dewan Rakyat sitting. There is no issue regarding that.” On March 14, Perikatan Nasional (PN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar said the coalition had decided to appoint a PAS MP as Opposition Leader following Hamzah’s expulsion from Bersatu on February 13.

items.title: Azam: Arrival of US$30m 1MDB-linked art haul in Malaysia kept secret for security reasons
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — The arrival time of four of 12 paintings linked to the misappropriation of 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) funds — valued at over US$30 million (RM120 million) — is being withheld for security reasons. Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said the decision was to safeguard the artworks, given their high value and their role as part of an ongoing investigation. However, Azam said the paintings have yet to be shipped as they are still undergoing documentation and logistical processes.

items.title: Perlis marks royal milestone with star-studded Silver Jubilee concert
items.summary: ARAU, April 5 — The Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail, graced the Perlis Royal Concert 2026 at Dewan Tuanku Canselor, Universiti Malaysia Perlis (UniMAP), last night, marking a key highlight of the Silver Jubilee celebrations commemorating his 25 years on the throne. Also gracing the event was the Raja Perempuan of Perlis, Tuanku Tengku Fauziah Almarhum Tengku Abdul Rashid. The Raja Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra Jamalullail, and the Raja Puan Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Hajah Lailatul Shahreen Akashah Khalil, as well as the second heir to the Perlis throne, Syed Sirajuddin Areeb Putra Al-Haj Jamalullail, were also in attendance.

items.title: Malaysia urges UN action after deadly attacks on Indonesian peacekeepers in Lebanon
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — Malaysia has condemned in no uncertain terms the continued attacks on United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (Unifil) peacekeepers in southern Lebanon which have resulted in deaths and injuries among UN personnel. In a statement issued today, the Foreign Ministry said the latest incident on April 3 in El Addaiseh injured three Indonesian peacekeepers. Malaysia urges the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) to take decisive action, including to ensure the immediate protection of UN peacekeepers in Southern Lebanon.

items.title: Storms to lash KL, Selangor, Perak, Negeri Sembilan and Sarawak until afternoon, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain, and strong winds affecting four states and the Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur until 1pm today. MetMalaysia, in a statement, said the affected areas in Perak are Bagan Datuk and Hilir Perak, while in Selangor, it involves Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Kuala Langat, and Hulu Langat. A similar weather warning also covers Jelebu in Negeri Sembilan.

items.title: Drowning incidents claim two in Pahang, rescuers resume search for missing boy
items.summary: KUANTAN, April 5 — A Form Two student and a fisherman who went missing in separate incidents at Kampung Durian Hijau and Pantai Sepat in Jerantut were found drowned yesterday. Mohd Salahuddin said the search operation at Pantai Sepat was called off after the victim was found, while the search at Kampung Durian Hijau for another missing victim, Muhammad Amirul Ashraf Abdullah, 14, will resume at 8am tomorrow. Meanwhile, Muhammad Danial Haikal Shamsudin and Muhammad Amirul Ashraf Abdullah were reported missing while swimming in Sungai Pahang at Kampung Durian Hijau.

items.title: Zero-waste Haj: Tabung Haji launches recycling drive for pilgrims’ ‘ihram’ garments
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 5 — Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) has introduced the ‘ Haji Lestari ’ initiative for the 1447H/2026 Haj season as an effort to cultivate a zero-waste culture among pilgrims through the management and recycling of used ihram garments. He noted that ihram garments worn by male pilgrims were typically kept or discarded after the Haj season. Pilgrims performing Haj and Umrah who wish to contribute used ihram garments may do so at selected TH branch offices nationwide starting October 2026.

items.title: Gas keeps Sabah steady, but diesel dependence a concern, says Hajiji
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 5 — Sabah’s gas-dominated energy supply structure has enabled the state to remain stable amid the global energy crisis following geopolitical conflict in West Asia, although its reliance on diesel for part of its electricity capacity remains a concern, according to Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor. He said the state’s current electricity generation is estimated at around 1,400 megawatts (MW), with the bulk of the supply provided through Petronas gas. If diesel prices rise, it will affect the situation in Sabah.

items.title: Diesel dependency leaves Sabah exposed to energy shocks, says DPM Fadillah
items.summary: KUCHING, April 5 — Sabah is set to face significant challenges in ensuring stable energy supply due to its heavy reliance on diesel for power generation, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof. He said rising diesel prices and global supply constraints are expected to put pressure on the state’s power generation capacity, potentially affecting domestic, industrial and business needs. Fadillah, who is also minister of energy transition and water transformation, said that in Peninsular Malaysia, energy generation relies largely on gas, which accounts for more than 30 per cent, as well as coal, whose supply is seen as more stable, although ensuring gas supply remains a priority.

items.title: Two drown after kayak capsizes in rough waters at Pantai Merdeka in Kedah
items.summary: Kuala Muda police chief ACP Hanyan Ramlan said the bodies of both victims were discovered at about 7.30pm. He said the victims were believed to be participants in a programme organised by a higher learning institution at the location. — Bernama

items.title: Zahid: Govt engaging industry leaders weekly to navigate Middle East energy uncertainty
items.summary: BAGAN DATUK, April 5 — The government is making every effort to find solutions to address the pressures arising from the impact of the global energy crisis following the conflict in West Asia, says Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. The Hari Raya Aidilfitri celebration hosted by the Bagan Datuk MP drew about 14,000 visitors. Also present were Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad, Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Zambry Abd Kadir, Deputy Home Minister Datuk Seri Shamsul Anuar Nasarah and Majlis Amanah Rakyat chairman Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki.

items.title: Madani Mart uses licensing model, not govt-funded, says deputy minister amid KR1M comparisons
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 5 — Madani Mart operates on a licensing model in collaboration with entrepreneurs and does not rely on government funding, said Deputy Minister of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Datuk Fuziah Salleh. Fuziah said Yayasan Madani, as the brand owner, manages the ecosystem, including training, systems, pricing and retail analysis supported by artificial intelligence (AI) She said the model gives entrepreneurs flexibility to run their businesses with supply chain support from Yayasan Madani. “Yayasan Madani, which is aligned with the government, does not receive government funding and does not provide capital for Madani Mart.

items.title: MACC nabs temple ex-chairman over RM50,000 claim for repairs that allegedly never happened
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has arrested a former chairman of a temple in the state over suspected misappropriation of temple funds worth RM50,000. According to an MACC source, the man, in his 70s, was arrested at 3pm last Thursday when he showed up to give his statement at the Selangor MACC office. Investigations are now centred on the temple, which was founded in the 1950s, and its major financial sources — the donations from Hindu devotees at the temple.
